content.full_text:
FPP TRANSMITTAL OF CORRESPONDENCE

CORRESPONDENT Allen Lopez, [illegible] Albuquerque                DATE REC'D 4/23
DATED 2/20          RE: Campaign [illegible] County

ADMINISTRATION               CONFLICTS/LEGAL              ENFORCEMENT
BOB T.                       TOM S.                       BOB B.
LINDA                        STELLA                       ROGER
BEV
                            BARBARA
EXECUTIVE                   DIANE                        TASA
TOM H.                       PATSY                        LYNN
TED                                                       CARLA
NANCY W.                                                  MARYANN
JEANNE                                                    BERMA
BOB S.                                                    JOHN

                            A-81-02-008

INFORMATION              REQUEST FOR ADVICE--MUST BE ANSWERED WITHIN 21 DAYS

Allen, PLEASE:

        TAKE WHATEVER ACTION YOU CONSIDER APPROPRIATE
        REPLY DIRECT BY 3/5
                          --COPY TO [illegible]
        PREPARE
        SUGGESTED REPLY AND RETURN TO
        COORDINATE WITH

3/4/81  Called M. Lopez; he returned my call-
        obtained the activities of the Committee w/
        m. lopez. I determined that the committee is
        a "primary purpose" committee and must file campaign
        disclosure statement. Mr. Lopez stated he would prepare
        the appropriate campaign statement for 1980 & 1981

                                                        JBC

HISPANIC DEMOCRATS OF ORANGE COUNTY

February 20, 1981

Mr. Rick Echman
Fair Political Practice Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Echman:

I was referred to you by the local office in Santa Ana to
explain to you what the Hispanic Democrats in Orange County
have been doing as far as our basic work and also to find
out if we have to give Fair Political Practice reports.

We have placed a slate for the last major election in the
hispanic newspapers in Orange County for the elections
last November. We also had 5,000 door hangers for Louis
Valesquez who ran for 35th Senatorial District and also for
Judge Orozco who ran for the Municipal Judgeship in Santa
Ana.

We also helped sponsor the democratic election night, co-
sponsored the democratic party on election night, and last
week we sponsored "Meet Your Candidate Night".

This is basically what our work consists of and it will be
sincerely appreciated if you could get back to me with an
answer within the next week regarding the Fair Political
Practice reports.

Thank you for your cooperation.

Sincerely,

HISPANIC DEMOCRATS OF ORANGE COUNTY

[signature]
Don Lopez
Treasurer
I.D. 802004

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804     1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

February 25, 1981

Don Lopez
Treasurer, Hispanic Democrats of Orange County
8530 Stanton Avenue
Buena Park, CA  90620

Dear Mr. Lopez:

The information provided in your letter dated February 20, 1981
is insufficient for me to determine your possible reporting
obligations under the Political Reform Act. Please call me at
(916) 322-5662 or submit a phone number at which you can be
reached during work hours.

Enclosed is a copy of the "Information Manual on Campaign
Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act" for your
review.

Cordially,

J. Richard Eichman
Accounting Specialist

Enclosure

FILE AN ORIGINAL AND _____ COPY OF THIS FORM WITH
Secretary of State
Political Reform Division
1230 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95807

STATEMENT OF ORGANIZATION (RECIPIENT COMMITTEE)
(Government Code Section 84103-84103)

[Type or Print in Ink]

NAME OF COMMITTEE:
HISPANIC DEMOCRATS OF ORANGE COUNTY

STREET ADDRESS OF COMMITTEE:
8550 Talton Ave.

Buena Park, Calif. 90620

DATE QUALIFIED AS A COMMITTEE (Mo., Day, Yr.): 7/14 1980

I     TREASURER AND OTHER PRINCIPAL OFFICERS

POSITION | NAME AND PERMANENT STREET ADDRESS | [AREA CODE] | PHONE NO.

TREASURER | DON S. LOPEZ (Chairman & Treasurer) | 714 828 8495
         | 9638 Paseo de Oro, Cypress, Ca. 90530 |            |
         |                                      |            |
         | Linda Godinez                        | 714 542 3899
         | 2219 N. Baker, Santa Ana, Ca. 92706 |            |

Attach additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheet.

II    IS THIS A CONTROLLED COMMITTEE?

(A controlled committee is one which is controlled directly or indirectly by a candidate or which acts jointly with a candidate.
A controlled committee has significant influence on the actions or decisions of the committee, or which acts jointly with a candidate.
A candidate controls a committee, if he, his agent or any committee controls, has significant influence on the actions or decisions of the committee.)

[ ] YES (Complete Section III)     [X] NO (Section III is not applicable)

III   CANDIDATES AND COMMITTEES BY WHICH THIS COMMITTEE IS CONTROLLED OR WITH WHICH
      THIS COMMITTEE ACTS JOINTLY

FOR OFFICIAL | NAME OF CANDIDATES OR COMMITTEE | IDENTIFICATION NUMBER OF COMMITTEE OR
USE ONLY     |                                 | TREASURER'S NAME AND PERMANENT STREET ADDRESS

Attach additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheet.

IV    ORGANIZATIONS, IF ANY, WITH WHICH THIS COMMITTEE IS AFFILIATED OR CONNECTED

NAME AND STREET ADDRESS | [AREA CODE] | PHONE NO.

NONE

Attach additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheet.

YOU MUST COMPLETE THE VERIFICATION ON PAGE 2.

For information required to be provided to you pursuant to the Information Practices Act of 1977, see "Information Memorandum on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act," Section XI.

- 1 -

CANDIDATES AND MEASURES SUPPORTED OR OPPOSED AS A PRINCIPAL ACTIVITY OF TI
COMMITTEE

CANDIDATE'S NAME, MEASURES TITLE
INCLUDING BALLOT NUMBER OR LETTER | SUPPORT | OPPOSE | CANDIDATES OR MEASURES LOCATION
(Include district number, city or county, as applicable.)

NONE

Attach additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheets.

VI COMMITTEE'S PRINCIPAL ACTIVITY WHEN NOT SUPPORTING OR OPPOSING SPECIFIC CANDIDATES
OR MEASURES

Voter Registration, Voter Education, Get out The Vote activities
at elections, Candidate development.

Attach additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheets.

VII IN THE EVENT OF TERMINATION, WHAT DISPOSITION WILL BE MADE OF SURPLUS FUNDS?

Charitable Contributions

VERIFICATION

I declare under penalty of perjury that to the best of my knowledge this statement is true, correct and complete and that I have used
reasonable diligence in its preparation.

Executed on 8/11/80 at Buena Park, Ca. by [illegible]
(DATE) (CITY AND STATE) (SIGNATURE)

- 2 -

embedding.qa_text: This document is a transmittal of correspondence dated 2/20, with the subject line "RE: Campaign finance." The correspondent is listed as "Hon. Lopez, Democratic of Orange County." The document lists several names under Administration, Conflicts/Legal, Enforcement, Executive, and TA&A, with some names checked off. The information section states: "REQUEST FOR ADVICE--MUST BE ANSWERED WITHIN 21 DAY." The request reads: "Rick, PLEASE: TAKE WHATEVER ACTION YOU CONSIDER APPROPRIATE REPLY DIRECT BY 3/9 --COPY TO [initially illegible] PREPARE [illegible] REPLY AND RETURN TO [illegible] COORDINATE REPLY WITH [illegible] 3/4/81 Called Mr. Lopez; he returned my call— Discussed the activities of the committee with Mr. Lopez. I determined that the committee is a "primary purpose" committee and must fill campaign disclosure statements. Mr. Lopez stated he would prepare the appropriate campaign statements for 1980 or 1981." The signature at the bottom is "G.R.E." February 20, 1981 Mr. Rick Echman Fair Political Practice Commission Post Office Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804 Dear Mr. Echman: I was referred to you by the local office in Santa Ana to explain to you what the Hispanic Democrats in Orange County have been doing as far as our basic work and also to find out if we have to give Fair Political Practice reports. We have placed a slate for the last major election in the hispanic newspapers in Orange County for the elections last November. We also had 5,000 door hangers for Louis Valesquez who ran for 35th Senatorial District and also for Judge Orozco who ran for the Municipal Judgeship in Santa Ana. We also helped sponsor the democratic election night, co-sponsored the democratic party on election night, and last week we sponsored "Meet Your Candidate Night". This is basically what our work consists of and it will be sincerely appreciated if you could get back to me with an answer within the next week regarding the Fair Political Practice reports. Thank you for your cooperation. Sincerely, HISPANIC DEMOCRATS OF ORANGE COUNTY Don Lopez Treasurer I.D. 802004 February 25, 1981 Don Lopez Treasurer, Hispanic Democrats of Orange County 8530 Stanton Avenue Buena Park, CA 90620 Dear Mr. Lopez: The information provided in your letter dated February 20, 1981 is insufficient for me to determine your possible reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act. Please call me at (916) 322-5662 or submit a phone number at which you can be reached during work hours. Enclosed is a copy of the "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act" for your review. Cordially, J. Richard Eichman Accounting Specialist Enclosure This document is a "Statement of Organization" form for the Hispanic Democrats of Orange County, filed with the Registrar of Voters in 1980. Here's a summary of the information provided: **FULL NAME OF COMMITTEE:** HISPANIC DEMOCRATS OF ORANGE COUNTY **STREET ADDRESS OF COMMITTEE:** 8530 Stanton Ave., Buena Park, Calif. 90620 **DATE QUALIFIED AS COMMITTEE (DAY, MONTH, YEAR):** AUG 18 1980 **TREASURER AND OTHER PRINCIPAL OFFICERS:** | POSITION | NAME AND PERMANENT STREET ADDRESS | AREA CODE
